case_title: SAMUEL RODRIGUEZ VS. LARRY FLYNT'S HUSTLER CLUB SAN FRANCISCO et al

docket_entries.description: REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF MOTION
OPPOSITION TO MTN TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF RODRIGUEZ, SAMUEL
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-25-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS UNCONTESTED. DEFENDANT SAW ENTERTAINMENT DBA LARRY FLYNT'S HUSTLER CLUB'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY ACTION IS GRANTED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT EXPLICITLY INCLUDES ANY AND ALL CLAIMS ARISING FROM THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP AND ARISING UNDER TORT LAW. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM FOR UNLAWFUL TERMINATION ARISES UNDER THE EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP AND THE CLAIMS FOR BATTERY AND THE RATIFICATION THEREOF ARISE UNDER TORT LAW, AND THEREFORE BOTH ARE WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. MOREOVER, BECAUSE THE PARTIES DESIGNATED THE FAA AS THE LAW GOVERNING THE ARBITRATION, CCP 1281.2(C) IS PREEMPTED BY FEDERAL LAW. THE SELECTION OF CALIFORNIA SUBSTANTIVE LAW AS THE LAW TO BE APPLIED BY THE ARBITRATOR DOES NOT AFFECT THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT TO FULLY INCORPORATE THE FAA, WHICH LACKS ANY PROVISION PERMITTING A TRIAL COURT TO REFUSE TO ENFORCE A VALID ARBITRATION AGREEMENT ON THE BASIS OF PENDING LITIGATION BETWEEN THE PLAINTIFF AND A NON-PARTY TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT. MOTION TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE COMPLAINT GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Continued To Dec-02-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Deft. Saw Entertainment Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RUSSELL I. GOODROW, TEL. NO. 655-9478 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JESSICA M. RYLAND OF THE HAIGHT LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 281-7619 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SAW ENTERTAINMENT;
ATTY. DOUGLAS MELTON OF LONG & LEVIT LLP, TEL. NO. 397-2222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PENTHOUSE CLUB.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Defendant Saw Entertainment'S Motion For Sanctions - Plaintiff's motion to terminate the arbitration and alternative motion to bifurcate the case is denied and SAW's motion for sanctions is granted each of plaintiff's two attorneys but not their client are required to pay a penalty of $50 to the court no later than May 29, 2015. The motion to terminate the arbitration and alternative motion to bifurcate the case is an impermissible and unacknowledged effort to seek reconsideration of prior rulings without complying with any of the requirements of the reconsideration statute and, therefore, the legal contentions therein run afoul of the certification requirement of section 128.7(b)(2). Effective deterrence of repeat conduct of this nature is best achieved by a monetary penalty against plaintiff's attorneys rather than any payment to SAW. 1 Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR # 12913 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Terminate Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Â§ 1281.2(C); Motion To Bifurcate Case Against Defendant Penthouse Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Â§ 1048(B)
Off Calendar Apr-24-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RUSSELL I. GOODROW, TEL. NO. 655-9478 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JESSICA M. RYLAND OF THE HAIGHT LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 281-7619 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SAW ENTERTAINMENT;
ATTY. DOUGLAS MELTON OF LONG & LEVIT LLP, TEL. NO. 397-2222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PENTHOUSE CLUB.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF SAMUEL RODRIGUEZ'S Motion To Terminate Arbitration Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sec. 1281.2(C); Motion To Bifurcate Case Against Defendant Penthouse Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sec. 1048(B) - Plaintiff's motion to terminate the arbitration and alternative motion to bifurcate the case is denied and SAW's motion for sanctions is granted each of plaintiff's two attorneys but not their client are required to pay a penalty of $50.00 to the court no later than May 29, 2015. The motion to terminate the arbitration and alternative motion to bifurcate the case is an impermissible and unacknowledged effort to seek reconsideration of prior rulings without complying with any of the requirements of the reconsideration statute and, therefore, the legal contentions therein run afoul of the certification requirement of section 128.7(b)(2). Effective deterrence of repeat conduct of this nature is best achieved by a monetary penalty against plaintiff's attorneys rather than any payment to SAW. 1 Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene L. Tofft, CSR # 12913 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2014 Continued To Dec-31-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
Off Calendar Mar-13-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Shane M. Cahill, Esq. of Long & Levit LLP, Tel. 415.438.4594, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANT SAW ENTERTAINMENT DBA LARRY FLYNT'S HUSTLER CLUB's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action is Granted. The arbitration agreement explicitly includes any and all claims arising from the employment relationship and arising under tort law. Plaintiff's claim for unlawful termination arises under the employment relationship and the claims for battery and the ratification thereof arise under tort law, and therefore both are within the scope of the arbitration agreement. Moreover, because the parties designated the FAA as the law governing the arbitration, CCP 1281.2(c) is preempted by federal law. The selection of California substantive law as the law to be applied by the arbitrator does not affect the parties' agreement to fully incorporate the FAA, which lacks any provision permitting a trial court to refuse to enforce a valid arbitration agreement on the basis of pending litigation between the Plaintiff and a non-party to the arbitration agreement. Motion to take judicial notice of the complaint granted. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.
